taxonomy: Ophiuros auriculatus is a species of brittle star, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Ophiuridae, which is part of the order Ophiurida. This order is within the class Ophiuroidea, a group of brittle stars that are characterized by their long, slender arms. The class Ophiuroidea is part of the subphylum Crinoidea, which includes all crinoids and brittle stars. The subphylum Crinoidea is part of the phylum Echinodermata, a diverse group of marine animals that also includes sea urchins and sea stars. At the highest level, the phylum Echinodermata is part of the kingdom Animalia.
